---
pagetitle: "crosslink — Persistent Memory for Human-Agent Development"
toc: false
sidebar: false
---

::: {.hero-section}

![](../images/banner.svg){.hero-banner}

# crosslink

::: {.subtitle}
You direct. Your agents remember. Nothing gets lost.
:::

::: {.cta-buttons}
[Get Started](getting-started/quickstart.qmd){.btn-primary}
[View on GitHub](https://github.com/forecast-bio/crosslink){.btn-secondary}
:::

:::

---

::: {.feature-cards}

::: {.feature-card}
### Session Memory
Your agents remember everything — handoff notes, breadcrumbs, and session state survive context compression and restarts. Pick up exactly where you left off.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Local-First Tracking
All data lives in a single SQLite file (`.crosslink/issues.db`). No cloud, no sync complexity, no network requests. Your project state stays on your machine.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Multi-Agent Coordination
Launch multiple agents on the same repo and let them sort it out. Distributed locking via a git coordination branch prevents conflicts automatically.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Behavioral Hooks
Your agents follow the rules without being told. Hooks inject best practices automatically — no stubs, proper error handling, issue tracking enforcement, and language-specific guidelines.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Swarm Orchestration
Point a swarm of agents at a design document. `crosslink swarm` coordinates phased builds with budget-aware scheduling, phase gates, and checkpoint/resume for interrupted work.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Knowledge Management
Research done by one agent is available to all. Shared markdown knowledge pages synced via git with full-text search, bulk import, and auto-injection into agent context.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Terminal Dashboard
`crosslink tui` gives you a live view of issues, active agents, knowledge pages, milestones, and config — all in your terminal with mouse support and keyboard navigation.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Web Dashboard
`crosslink web` launches a browser-based dashboard for visual project oversight. Same data, richer interface — charts, drag-and-drop, and real-time agent monitoring.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Container Agents
Run agents in isolated Docker containers for stronger sandboxing. Same hook enforcement, same coordination — works cross-platform.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Smart Workflow
Tell your agent what to fix and it handles the rest — `quick` creates, labels, and starts work in one command. `next` recommends what to tackle. `tree` visualizes your issue hierarchy.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Maintenance
`crosslink prune` cleans up stale hub and knowledge history. `crosslink maintain` runs health checks, compacts the database, and keeps your project lean.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Works Everywhere
Native CLI + VS Code extension. Integrates with Claude Code, Aider, Cursor, Continue.dev, or any AI agent via the context provider.
:::

:::

## How It Works

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}
**You say:**

> "Fix the auth token refresh bug"

Your agent handles everything — creates a tracked issue, explores the code, implements the fix, runs tests, and leaves handoff notes for the next session.
:::
::: {.column width="10%"}
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
**Your agent executes:**

```bash
crosslink quick "Fix auth token refresh" -p high -l bug
crosslink session action "Exploring auth module..."
# ... implements, tests, commits ...
crosslink session end --notes "Fixed. PR ready."
```
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Manual CLI equivalent"}
```bash
crosslink session start
crosslink quick "Fix auth token refresh" -p high -l bug
# ... do the work yourself ...
crosslink session end --notes "Fixed."
```
:::

## Install

```bash
# From crates.io
cargo install crosslink

# Or build from source
git clone https://github.com/forecast-bio/crosslink.git
cd crosslink/crosslink && cargo install --path .
```

Also available as a [VS Code extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=forecast-bio.crosslink-issue-tracker).

## Learn More

- [Your First Agent Session](getting-started/quickstart.qmd) — get running in under a minute
- [Installation](getting-started/installation.qmd) — all install methods and requirements
- [Session Workflow](guides/session-workflow.qmd) — deep dive into session management
- [Kickoff: Autonomous Agents](guides/kickoff.qmd) — launch background agents in worktrees
- [Swarm Orchestration](guides/swarm.qmd) — multi-agent phased builds from design documents
- [Knowledge Management](guides/knowledge.qmd) — shared research pages synced via git
- [Web Dashboard](guides/web-dashboard.qmd) — browser-based project oversight
- [Terminal Dashboard](guides/tui.qmd) — interactive TUI for issues and agents
- [Maintenance](guides/maintenance.qmd) — pruning, health checks, and database compaction
- [CLI Reference](reference/commands.qmd) — full command reference
